title: Spain & France 2008: Bilbao Ferry, Burgos, Zamora, Salamanca, Madrid & the Pyrenees

main_route.excerpt: Simon, Charles & Eve set off for Spain & France. We went down the M5 & changed to the M4 near Swindon.
In Savernake Forest we had lunch in the shade. Then we went on to Venetia's for 3.30.

main_route.inference_reason: UK ferry port not named in the diary. They left Venetia's (reached via the M4 near Swindon, Wiltshire) at 6pm for the ferry and spent a long time on the quay before a multi-night Bay of Biscay crossing to Bilbao (dolphins/whale, two days at sea). The 2008 Bilbao car-ferry from southern England was P&O's Portsmouth-Bilbao service, so portsmouth-bilbao is used; the only Bilbao route in ferries.json is portsmouth-bilbao.
main_route.excerpt: At 6 we left for the ferry. We seemed to spend hours on the quai. We ate supper as we waited for the ship to leave port, on deck

main_route.inference_reason: Spanish port not named explicitly, but the ferry landed at the start of Tuesday and they immediately stopped at Amurrio (Amurria) before lunch in Burgos — Amurrio lies on the road from Bilbao towards Burgos. The trip is the Bilbao ferry crossing; Santander would not pass through Amurrio.
main_route.excerpt: At a truly horrendous early hour (6) we had breakfast & left the ferry at 7. We meandered along looking at the plains at Amurria we stopped & walked around.
We meandered along looking at the plains at Amurria we stopped & walked around. We shopped at a supermarket & had lunch in Burgos
On to a campsite across the river from Burgos.
We slept in Zamora campsite + drove into Zamora the next day, parking across the river. The Cathedral has an amazing, fragile-looking dome.
We have arrived at an extraordinary campsite Camping Torres by a lake near Salamanca. Everything that can be wrong is wrong + unwelcoming. It reminds Simon of Fawlty Towers.
We drove to Arapile & Simon & Eve walked up a ridge to where Wellington stood... he sprang an attack & decimated 40,000 French soldiers in 40 minutes.
On we went to Avila. The heat caught up with us, so we didn't climb the walls. We saw the cathedral.

main_route.original_wording: a campsite which is as well run as the last one was badly run
main_route.inference_reason: A lakeside campsite reached after Ávila, popular with people from Madrid, where the family stayed Saturday-Monday (two nights, swimming in the lake daily). The campsite is unnamed and its exact location is not given; anchored to the Ávila area / Sierra de Gredos region from which they next drove to Toledo.
main_route.excerpt: We drove on to a campsite which is as well run as the last one was badly run... we went for a swim in the lovely [?lake]... The camp is packed cheek-by-jowl. People from Madrid with their microwaves + fridges in rented caravans.
We reached Toledo & stayed at the Cardinal Hostal, a really lovely hotel, an old house belonging to a cardinal, wood, metal, tiles. Marvellous air con. Outside it was 40°.
We drove on to Madrid. Susana + Eugenio were most welcoming + we stayed in their beautiful flat... We walked to the Prado where they showed us their favourite pictures.

main_route.normalized_name: Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo, Soria, Spain
main_route.original_wording: El Frontal, near the Embalse de la Cuerta del Pozo, in the hills of Orbion
main_route.excerpt: We are near the source of the R. Aouro, El Frontal **** near the Embalse de la Cuerta del Pozo. in the hills of Orbion... I swam (Simon + Charles paddled) in the enormous lake.
We visited the Laguna Negra. Someone has written a poem about it. Really lovely. In over the pass of Puerta de Santa Inés, which is closed in winter.

main_route.normalized_name: Santa María de Eunate, Navarre, Spain
main_route.excerpt: We stopped at St. Maria Eurate (again) on the pilgrimage route. There's a stone colonnade around it.

main_route.original_wording: Camping Ezcaba, north of Pamplona, near Suaren
main_route.excerpt: We reached Camping Ezcaba [illegible] north of [?A]Pamplona. It's near Suaren, where Wellington fought. It's about the best campsite we've been in.
We drove to look at the Bridge at Sorauren where Wellington wrote to ask for reinforcements - Paet's group. Very very hilly.
We drove through Roncevalles, + the the town, because there were no parking places, but we stopped at the pass which had beautiful views. The police were checking for terrorist bombs (ETA etc).
We arrived at a marvellous campsite, Cave de Sauveterre de Bearn ***** friendly + a lovely river to wade through + swim in.
reached Queyssac campsite, ***** 7k north of Bergerac, by evening, Charles' favourite campsite. It was open, good views, a lake with ducks.
We drove off north towards David's house. We lunched by a river in the rain + then went to the Tree Museum at Chamberet.

main_route.inference_reason: David's house, the fixed base where they reunited with Thomas, Naomi & Austin. Place name uncertain ([?Sas Noux] / 'Sas Noux' / 'Bas Noux' / 'Noux' in the index list). It lies north of Bergerac and is reached after the Tree Museum at Chamberet (Corrèze); the route index also lists 'Tregniac' (Treignac) near 'Noux, David's house', so anchored to the Treignac/Chamberet area of the Corrèze.
main_route.excerpt: We arrived at [?Sard's] house, [?Sas Noux], at 4, just after Thomas + [illegible] Naomi + Austin.
Simon + Eve + Charles left. We stopped in Maymac at + shopped at a good green grocer.

We reached St. Jean de Vaux where it continued to feel very cold, so we didn't swim... For the first time the restaurant in the village was open, so we had a meal Café du Centre... We also tasted wine for William's + Bryony's wedding.

We picked up the 60 bottles of wine, at 9.30, + some others + set off northwards. We shopped + drove on until 6, arriving at an almost deserted campsite with one other van. It's a bit warmer. Ribemont **

main_route.inference_reason: All family trips start and end at home. The surviving pages end mid-trip at Ribemont (Aisne, northern France) with the family heading north laden with wine for William & Bryony's wedding; the Channel crossing and return home are not recorded in the transcribed pages.

crossings.inference_reason: Outbound crossing. Neither port is named, but it is a multi-night Bay of Biscay ferry (supper on deck waiting to leave port; dolphins and a whale at sea over two days) landing near Bilbao — the first Spanish stop is Amurrio, on the Bilbao-Burgos road. The 2008 southern-England-to-Bilbao car ferry was P&O's Portsmouth-Bilbao service, the only Bilbao route in ferries.json.
crossings.excerpt: we had breakfast & left the ferry at 7. We meandered along looking at the plains at Amurria we stopped & walked around.

excursions.label: Day trip from Zamora to San Pedro de la Nave

stops.normalized_name: San Pedro de la Nave, Zamora, Spain
stops.original_wording: S. Pedro / San Pedro de la Nave
stops.excerpt: We had lunch in town, pork chops, & then drove about 17 miles to S. Pedro, a C- & church... It had been moved up, stone by stone, to a higher village in 1930's when they flooded the valleys.

excursions.label: Day walk from David's house to St. Hilaire

stops.excerpt: Charles put in 2 chickens while the rest of us were on a super 12 mile walk, with a beer at St. Hilaire.

unresolved.original_wording: Venetia's (house, reached via the M4 near Swindon)
unresolved.issue: An evening tea stop on the departure day before the ferry — family/friends' house, location not specified beyond being reached off the M4 in the Swindon/Wiltshire area. Not geocodable to a stop; recorded only as context for inferring the Portsmouth ferry port.
unresolved.excerpt: Then we went on to Venetia's for 3.30... At 6 we left for the ferry.

unresolved.issue: Departure-day side stop to see 'Conta's' new house ('Simon said Conta lives over there'). A small hamlet (likely Hinton Parva near Swindon, Wiltshire); a brief visit, not an overnight stop. Left out of the main route as a minor pre-ferry diversion.
unresolved.excerpt: when we passed "Hunton Parva" I said "Let's go & see her new house. After much searching, we found it."

unresolved.issue: River/place name near Zamora associated with the Wellington 1812 narrative ('crossed the river, deep ravines, to get onto the north bank of the [?Anero]'). Spelling uncertain; likely a tributary of the Douro/Esla rather than a stop the family visited.
unresolved.excerpt: crossed the river, deep ravines, to get onto the north bank of the [?Anero]. The French didn't think it could be done

boundary_flags: Several pages bound within this spread range belong to OTHER trips and were excluded: B_0142_R (no.80, dated '10/06.07.88', Beaune / Auvergne / [?Fridefont] — a 1988 entry); B_0143_L (no date, 'Friday 15', submerged-village dam and Gorges de Ses) and B_0143_R (no.40, Mallet beach / August bread festival) — a separate trip; B_0150_L (a printed museum brochure on French dolls and glass).

B_0145_L, B_0145_R and B_0146_L are a long numbered index/route list spanning MANY trips (items 1-13 match this 2008 Spain trip — Zamora, St Pedro, Salamanca, Avila, Toledo, Madrid, Embalse, Pamplona/Suaren, Roncesvalles, Bergerac, David's house, St Jean de Vaux — but items 14 onward cover unrelated UK, Belgium, Germany and Lot trips). Treated as supporting evidence for ordering, NOT as a single-trip sketch.
B_0144_L is an address/contacts page (the Edgbaston home address; the Triana family's Prado note) — contextual, not a stop.
Page numbering restarts mid-trip: pages run no.76-80 (B_0138_R..B_0141_R) then a fresh sequence no.1-12 (B_0146_R..B_0157_R), reflecting a new notebook/volume started during the trip.
Trip is incomplete: the transcribed pages end at Ribemont with the family driving north in France; the return Channel crossing and arrival home are not recorded.
summary: A long summer 2008 tour of Spain and France by Simon, Eve and their son Charles (19), departing Birmingham on Sunday 27 July. They drove south via Savernake Forest to a friend's house in Wiltshire, then took an overnight Bay of Biscay ferry (P&O Portsmouth-Bilbao) while Thomas, Naomi and Austin drove their own van over the Pyrenees. Landing near Bilbao, the family toured Castile and León — Burgos, Zamora (with San Pedro de la Nave), Salamanca, the Wellington battlefield at Arapiles, Ávila, Toledo and Madrid (staying with the Triana family) — following Wellington's Peninsular War campaign and visiting lakeside campsites in the Sierra de Urbión. They returned north via Pamplona (Sorauren), Roncesvalles and into France, camping at Sauveterre-de-Béarn and near Bergerac before reuniting with Thomas's family at David's house in the Corrèze, then heading on through Burgundy (St-Jean-de-Vaux) to collect wine for William and Bryony's wedding; the surviving pages end at Ribemont in northern France.
extraction_notes: Travellers confirmed repeatedly as Simon, Eve and Charles (19) — 'Simon, Eve & Charles (19) are in Spain' (B_0146_R); they travelled separately from Thomas, Naomi (variously 'Nadin'/'Naomi') and baby Austin, who crossed the Pyrenees by road and met them at David's house. Neither ferry port is named; portsmouth-bilbao is inferred from the multi-night Biscay crossing, the trip_id ('bilbao-ferry'), and the first Spanish stop being Amurrio on the Bilbao-Burgos road. Date inconsistencies in the diary: the entry headed 'Monday 5th August' (B_0152_R) follows a Saturday/Sunday at the Ávila-area campsite — in 2008 Monday was 4 August — so weekday and date drift apart; date_precision kept at 'day' for the confidently dated outbound days (27-31 July, 1 August) but most later dates are weekday-only and approximate. The lakeside campsite near Ávila (seq 11) and David's house (seq 23) are unnamed/uncertain and marked approximate. diary_page_range is [76, 12] reflecting the mid-trip restart of page numbering (no.76 at the start, ending at no.12). Excluded out-of-trip pages and the multi-trip index list are detailed in boundary_flags.
